title: Processors implement IProcessor with onOperations receiving OperationWithContext items filtered by document type, scope, branch, and document ID
content: A processor implements `IProcessor` with `onOperations(operations: OperationWithContext[])` and `onDisconnect()`. Each `OperationWithContext` pairs an `Operation` (action type, input, index, hash, timestamp) with `OperationContext` (documentId, documentType, scope, branch, ordinal for cross-document ordering, resultingState as JSON). `ProcessorFilter` has optional arrays: `documentType`, `scope`, `branch`, `documentId` (use `["*"]` for all). The factory pattern: outer function receives `IProcessorHostModule` (shared resources), inner function receives `driveHeader` per drive, returns `ProcessorRecord[]`. By default processors replay all history; set `startFrom: "current"` to skip historical operations.
description: ProcessorFilter determines which ops a processor receives; factory creates instances per drive with configurable filters.
